title: Fast-Pass Pricing on the Far Side of the Crowd
strategies: trivial: post the given cheap reference price on every ride (floods the fast lanes)
greedy: per-ride static demand-curve pricing assuming an empty fast lane (ignores crowding + budgets, underprices, floods)
strong: reparametrize each ride by equilibrium buyer count F, price at the marginal-indifference price p(F)=v_[F]*Savings(F), keep the lane scarce -- prices ABOVE the static-demand optimum
headroom: jointly account for the cross-ride cardinality-budget coupling (visitors juggle at most K passes), which the per-ride strong solution ignores
